sections.paragraph:
πλάνος, ον (πλανάω; Trag. et al.; LXX, Philo, Joseph.) in our lit. only in the transf. sense.: pert. to causing someone to be mistaken

ⓐ as adj., leading astray, deceitful (so Menand., Fgm. 288 Kö.; Theocr. 21, 43; Moschus 1, 28; 5, 10; Jos., Bell. 2, 259; Just., A II, 15, 1, D. 70, 5 al.) πνεύματα πλάνα deceitful spirits 1 Ti 4:1 (cp. TestBenj 6:1; Just., D. 7, 3 al.) τούτων εἰδώλων τῶν πλάνων these seductive images ApcPt Bodl.

ⓑ as subst. ὁ πλάνος deceiver, impostor (Diod S 34 + 35, Fgm. 2, 14; Vett. Val. 74, 18; Ps.-Clem., Hom. 4, 2) of Jesus Mt 27:63 (cp. TestLevi 16:3). W. ὁ ἀντίχριστος 2J 7b; pl. vs. 7a. ὡς πλάνοι καὶ ἀληθεῖς considered impostors, and (yet are) true 2 Cor 6:8.—DELG s.v. πλανάομαι. M-M. TW.

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

transf. transf. = transferred

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

adj. adj. = adjective

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Theocr Theocr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Moschus Moschus , bucol., II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)
  2. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

TestBenj TestBenj = Testament of Benjamin, s. Test12Patr—List 2

ApcPt Bodl. ApcPt Bodl. = Apocalypse of Peter, Bodleian—List 1

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

  1. pl. = plural

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 822
